metadata.title: Build a File-Rename Dry-Run Tool with Claude
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. The confirmation gate is not a UX choice — it's the only safety guarantee the tool has.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. The confirmation gate is not a UX choice — it's the only safety guarantee the tool has.

beats.narration_text: Rename operations are CoWork-tier: multi-step, requires human review at the manifest stage, irreversible on execution. A tool without a mandatory dry-run skips the review moment — which means it skips the only opportunity to catch errors before they become permanent.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Rename operations are CoWork-tier: multi-step, requires human review at the manifest stage, irreversible on execution. A tool without a mandatory dry-run skips

beats.narration_text: Given these filenames, propose a consistent naming convention and generate a rename manifest. Do not rename anything yet.

props.command: claude "Given these filenames, propose a consistent naming convention and generate a rename manifest in the format: OLD_NAME -> NEW_NAME [rationale]. Do not rename anything yet." < filelist.txt

beats.narration_text: The rename tool generates a manifest, shows a diff with old names in red and new names in green, requires explicit confirmation before executing any rename, and catches conflicts where two files would rename to the same target.

Manifest shows 12 renames: 10 clean, 1 conflict detected — two files would rename to report-2024.pdf — and 1 ambiguous case. The conflict is caught before any file is touched. Human reviews, resolves the conflict manually, then confirms execution.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Manifest shows 12 renames: 10 clean, 1 conflict detected — two files would rename to report-2024.pdf — and 1 ambiguous case. The conflict is caught before any f

beats.narration_text: Introduce a conflict intentionally — two files renaming to the same target — confirm conflict detection catches it.

Conflict detected: Q1_final.pdf and Q1_draft.pdf both resolve to q1-report.pdf. Execution blocked. Conflict report shows both sources and asks for human resolution. No file has been renamed. The gate held.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Conflict detected: Q1_final.pdf and Q1_draft.pdf both resolve to q1-report.pdf. Execution blocked. Conflict report shows both sources and asks for human resolut

beats.narration_text: The manifest-first pattern is plan before execute applied to the filesystem. The review moment is not bureaucracy — it's the only point where a human can catch naming conflicts, ambiguities, and scope errors before they become permanent file system state.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The manifest-first pattern is plan before execute applied to the filesystem. The review moment is not bureaucracy — it's the only point where a human can catch

beats.narration_text: Next: extract structured data from receipts — and show why schema matters.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Next: extract structured data from receipts — and show why schema matters.

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.
